posts.title: 🎓 Meet with an AHC Counselor at PVHS – Apr 6
posts.summary: Repeating sign-up event. Scan the QR code to reserve your time with an Allan Hancock College Counselor before Tuesday, April 21st.
posts.author: Melani Mederos
posts.body:
Hey Panthers!

If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, and you haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, scan the QR code below to reserve your appointment time for Tuesday, April 21st.

You must meet with an AHC Counselor to get your "First Semester Plan" so you can register for classes in May!

Questions? Please see your PVHS Counselor, Mr. Hernandez in the library, or Mrs. Mederos in the College & Career Center (room 604).

Repeating event — weekly until April 21, 2026 on Mondays. This occurrence: Mon Apr 06 – Sat Apr 25.

posts.title: 🚨 Campus Update – Student Medical Emergency
posts.summary: About noon today, a male PVHS student had a medical emergency during class. A school nurse responded immediately and the student was transported by ambulance.
posts.author: Paul Robinson, Principal
posts.body:
Good Afternoon PV School Community,

About noon today, a male PVHS student had a medical emergency during class. A school nurse immediately responded to the classroom and the student was transported by ambulance to the hospital for followup.

Counseling services are always made available during these types of concerning situations.

Respectfully,
Paul Robinson, Principal

posts.title: 🎪 Spring Fair, Minimum Day & Senior Updates – Tomorrow 4/2
posts.summary: Minimum day ends at 1:35 PM, Spring Fair during lunch, and seniors have panoramic photos + cap & gown pickup during periods 3–4.
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
Dear Families and Students,

We have an action-packed day tomorrow as we head into our Spring Break! Please review the following updates.

🕒 1. Minimum Day Schedule
Tomorrow will be a minimum day. School will conclude after Period 7 at 1:35 PM. Please ensure transportation arrangements are made for this earlier dismissal time.

🎡 2. Spring Fair Tomorrow
We are hosting our Spring Fair tomorrow during lunch. Our school clubs will be out in force selling a variety of food and treats. It's going to be a great day to enjoy the outdoors and support our student organizations.

🎓 3. Essential Updates for Seniors (Class of 2026)
Activities during Periods 3 and 4:

  • Panoramic Picture – Seniors head to the stands for their official group photo.
  • Graduation Rehearsal – First graduation walkthrough.
  • Cap & Gown Distribution – Jostens will be on-site immediately following the photo.

Attendance is critical. We want every senior in the picture and receiving their graduation gear on time!

🏖️ 4. Spring Break Reminder
Spring Break begins Friday, April 3 (No School). Classes resume Monday, April 13.

posts.title: 🎤 Motivational Keynote: Erica Alfaro – Mon 4/13 @ 6PM in PVHS Library
posts.summary: Award-winning author and keynote speaker Erica Alfaro presents at PVHS on Monday April 13 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. Free event for the whole community!
posts.author: Paul Robinson, Principal
posts.body:
Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library.

PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is very excited to offer our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message from Erica Alfaro. Erica is an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate.

In 2019, Erica's graduation pictures went viral and made headlines around the world — featured on CNN, The Huffington Post, and Yahoo News. Her book Harvesting Dreams won the 2022 International Latino Book Award in the Most Inspirational Young Adult category.

Don't miss this wonderful opportunity to be inspired!

posts.title: 🏈 Mandatory Football Meeting for 9th Graders
posts.summary: All 9th graders wanting to play football next fall must attend a mandatory meeting Tuesday, March 31 at 12:35 PM in Room 551 (by the cafeteria).
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
Calling all 9th graders ready to suit up for football next season! If you want to hit the field next fall, this is the meeting you can't miss — it's mandatory.

  • When: Tuesday, March 31
  • Time: 12:35 PM (Don't be late!)
  • Where: Room 551 (Right by the cafeteria)
  • What to bring: Your lunch and your "A" game.

Let's get the season started right. See you there!

— Coach Payne

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter – March 27, 2026
posts.summary: This week: Fiddler on the Roof opens at the Clark Center, new teacher hires, ELPAC testing wrap-up, Spring Fair announcement, and a Diablo Canyon Nuclear Plant field trip.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE — PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL
March 27, 2026

Hello Panthers! Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter.

🎭 Fiddler on the Roof
PVHS Center Stage rocked the Clark Center with their opening night performance. Guest Artist J.J. Niemann (Hamilton) joined PVHS performers after the show. Remaining performances: Mar 27 at 7pm, Mar 28 at 2pm & 7pm at Clark Center. Also April 16, 17, and 18 at 7pm in the Performing Arts Center.

👔 Hiring Update
PVHS has hired two International Language (Spanish) Teachers and one Science teacher. Multiple Special Education (MMSN) openings remain.

📋 ELPAC Testing
Wrapping up ELPAC testing — if you received a reminder call, please see Ms. Goldin as soon as possible.

🎡 Spring Fair & Senior Assembly
Spring Fair is next Thursday during lunch. Seniors will have their Graduation Assembly during periods 3 and 4 — come dressed to impress for panoramic and class year photos!

🎓 CCGI College Platform
Counselors visited 9th, 10th, and 11th grade classrooms for California College Guidance Initiative (CCGI) sessions — career assessments, college major alignment, and campus research.

⚛️ Diablo Canyon Field Trip
AP Environmental Science students visited Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant (provides ~10% of California's power), which recently reopened for student visits. A unique and memorable experience!

posts.title: 🎓 Senior Class Meeting – Thursday April 2 (Periods 3–4)
posts.summary: Class of 2026! Senior meeting during 3rd period on April 2. Panoramic photo, graduation info, and cap & gown pickup for those who already ordered.
posts.author: Lisa Walters
posts.body:
CLASS OF 2026!!

Next Thursday, April 2, there will be a senior class meeting beginning during 3rd period. You will go to your 3rd period class and then be dismissed to the gym.

During the meeting you will:

  • Have your panoramic and class year pictures taken
  • Receive information about end-of-year events
  • Pick up your cap and gown (if already ordered) at the end of the meeting

If you haven't ordered your cap and gown, go to jostens.com to order.

Reminder: Caps cannot be decorated or modified in any way.

posts.title: 📝 PSAT 10 Offered for Sophomores – Sign Up by March 27
posts.summary: 10th graders can take the PSAT on Wednesday April 22 (check-in at 7:30 AM). Sign up with your counselor or Mrs. Mederos in Room 604. Deadline: March 27.
posts.author: Israel Hernandez
posts.body:
📝 Opportunity for 10th Graders: PSAT Registration

Help your student get a head start on their college journey! The PSAT will be administered to our 10th grade students next month — a low-stakes way to practice the SAT format, identify strengths, and get comfortable with college entrance exams.

🗓️ Details

  • Test Date: Wednesday, April 22nd
  • Check-in Time: 7:30 AM
  • Who: All interested 10th grade students

✍️ How to Register

  • Visit your assigned School Counselor
  • See Mrs. Mederos in the College and Career Center (Room 604)

🚨 Hard Deadline: Friday, March 27th.

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter – March 20, 2026
posts.summary: This week: 7C's lesson design training, FFA regional leadership win, science field trip to the Central Coast Aquarium, and 4 First Place wins at State Thespian Festival.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE — PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL
March 20, 2026

Hello Panthers! Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter.

📚 7C's Lesson Design Training
PVHS Integrated Math 2 Teachers attended an all-day training for the 7C's Lesson Design: Constructing Engaging Lessons, Considering Students' Needs, Captivating Students, Chunking Learning, Connecting Learning, Checking for Understanding, and Carrying Learning into the Future.

🌾 FFA News
PV's Lucas Rodriguez will be the Regional Sentinel, attending the four-day regional leadership conference this summer. We are very proud of him!

🔬 Science Field Trip
PVHS Science teachers took students to the Central Coast Aquarium — hands-on learning applying classroom knowledge to a real-world setting.

🎭 State Thespian Festival – 4 Awards!

  • 1st Place – Solo Musical (300+ performers): Mariah Escobar
  • 2nd Place – Duet Musical: Jesus Manriquez & Serenity Castillo
  • 1st Place – Night 1 Thespians Got Talent: Nickolas McCormick
  • 1st Place – Night 2 Thespians Got Talent: Jesus Manriquez & Key Lovato

📱 Tardy Sweeps & Minga Hall Passes
Rolling out unannounced tardy sweeps and the Minga digital hall pass app. All students must download Minga from the App Store or Google Play.

🎓 CCGI College Platform
Counselors worked with freshmen, sophomores, and juniors on the California College Guidance Initiative (CCGI) — skills assessments, career exploration, and college/major searches.
